member.name: T:HumanFortress.Core.Commands.CommandQueue
member.summary: Deterministic command queue for input handling per UI_AND_INPUT_MODEL.md. Commands are tagged with target tick and executed in order.

member.name: M:HumanFortress.Core.Commands.CommandQueue.Enqueue(HumanFortress.Core.Commands.ICommand)
member.summary: Enqueue a command for execution. Thread-safe.

member.name: M:HumanFortress.Core.Commands.CommandQueue.ExecuteCommands(System.UInt64,HumanFortress.Core.Commands.ISimulationContext)
member.summary: Execute all commands scheduled for the given tick.

member.name: M:HumanFortress.Core.Commands.CommandQueue.GetExecutedCommands
member.summary: Get all executed commands for replay/save.

member.name: M:HumanFortress.Core.Commands.CommandQueue.ClearExecutedCommands
member.summary: Clear executed commands (after save).

member.name: M:HumanFortress.Core.Commands.CommandQueue.RestoreCommands(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{HumanFortress.Core.Commands.ICommand})
member.summary: Restore commands from save/replay.

member.name: T:HumanFortress.Core.Commands.ICommand
member.summary: Base interface for all game commands per UI_AND_INPUT_MODEL.md. Commands are the only way for UI to affect simulation state.

member.name: P:HumanFortress.Core.Commands.ICommand.Tick
member.summary: Tick when this command should be executed.

member.name: P:HumanFortress.Core.Commands.ICommand.CommandId
member.summary: Unique identifier for replay and debugging.

member.name: P:HumanFortress.Core.Commands.ICommand.CommandType
member.summary: Command type for serialization.

member.name: M:HumanFortress.Core.Commands.ICommand.Execute(HumanFortress.Core.Commands.ISimulationContext)
member.summary: Execute the command within the simulation context.

member.name: M:HumanFortress.Core.Commands.ICommand.Serialize
member.summary: Serialize command data for save/replay.

member.name: T:HumanFortress.Core.Commands.ISimulationContext
member.summary: Context provided to commands during execution.

member.name: T:HumanFortress.Core.Commands.IWorldReader
member.summary: Read-only world access for commands.

member.name: T:HumanFortress.Core.Content.ContentRegistry
member.summary: Central registry for all data-driven content per CONTENT_REGISTRY_OVERVIEW.md. Loads materials, geology, items, buildables, etc. from JSON with validation.

member.name: M:HumanFortress.Core.Content.ContentRegistry.LoadContent(System.String)
member.summary: Load all content from the content directory structure.

member.name: M:HumanFortress.Core.Content.ContentRegistry.GetZoneDefinition(System.String)
member.summary: Get zone definition data by ID.

member.name: T:HumanFortress.Core.Content.MaterialData
member.summary: Material data from JSON per MATERIALS_SPEC.md.

member.name: T:HumanFortress.Core.Content.GeologyData
member.summary: Geology/terrain data from JSON.

member.name: T:HumanFortress.Core.Content.FixedPoint
member.summary: Fixed-point math utilities for performance-critical material calculations. Per MATERIALS_SPEC v4-min: FX = 10_000 (1.0000) All dimensionless values (multipliers, resist multipliers, 0..100 scales) use FX integers. Only density remains in physical units (kg/m³ == mg/mL numerically).

member.name: F:HumanFortress.Core.Content.FixedPoint.FX
member.summary: Fixed-point scale: 1.0000 = 10000

member.name: F:HumanFortress.Core.Content.FixedPoint.FxHalf
member.summary: Half of FX for rounding and centering calculations

member.name: M:HumanFortress.Core.Content.FixedPoint.One
member.summary: Returns FX representation of 1.0

member.name: M:HumanFortress.Core.Content.FixedPoint.FromFloat(System.Double)
member.summary: Convert float to FX integer (rounded) Example: 1.5 → 15000, 0.25 → 2500

member.name: M:HumanFortress.Core.Content.FixedPoint.FromPct(System.Double)
member.summary: Convert percentage (0..100) to FX integer Example: 50 → 5000 (0.5), 100 → 10000 (1.0)

member.name: M:HumanFortress.Core.Content.FixedPoint.ToFloat(System.Int32)
member.summary: Convert FX integer back to float (for display/debugging)

member.name: M:HumanFortress.Core.Content.FixedPoint.ToPct(System.Int32)
member.summary: Convert FX integer back to percentage

member.name: M:HumanFortress.Core.Content.FixedPoint.Mul(System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Multiply two FX integers (rounded) Example: fx_mul(5000, 15000) = 0.5 * 1.5 = 0.75 = 7500

member.name: M:HumanFortress.Core.Content.FixedPoint.Div(System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Divide two FX integers (rounded) Example: fx_div(5000, 20000) = 0.5 / 2.0 = 0.25 = 2500

member.name: M:HumanFortress.Core.Content.FixedPoint.Dev(System.Int32)
member.summary: Center a 0..FX value around 0: returns [-FX/2, +FX/2] Used for material signals where 50 is neutral (e.g., hardness-50) Example: Dev(5000) = 0 (neutral), Dev(7500) = +2500 (above neutral)

member.name: M:HumanFortress.Core.Content.FixedPoint.NormFull(System.Int32)
member.summary: Normalize 0..FX to [-FX, +FX] for strong signals (optional)

member.name: M:HumanFortress.Core.Content.FixedPoint.Clamp(System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Clamp value to range [min, max]

member.name: M:HumanFortress.Core.Content.FixedPoint.Clamp(System.Double,System.Double,System.Double)
member.summary: Clamp double value to range [min, max]

member.name: M:HumanFortress.Core.Content.FixedPoint.ISqrt(System.Int32)
member.summary: Integer square root (for power calculations without floats)

member.name: M:HumanFortress.Core.Content.FixedPoint.Lerp(System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Linear interpolation between two FX integers t is 0..FX representing 0.0..1.0

member.name: M:HumanFortress.Core.Content.FixedPoint.InterpolateLUT(System.Int32[],System.Int32[],System.Int32)
member.summary: Interpolate from a lookup table (LUT) for expensive functions like pow() ratioPointsFx and valuePointsFx must be sorted by ratio

member.name: T:HumanFortress.Core.Content.MaterialIdRegistry
member.summary: Registry of material IDs for use in TileBase.GeoMatId. These IDs map to materials and geology entries in JSON files.

member.name: M:HumanFortress.Core.Content.MaterialIdRegistry.GetDisplay(System.UInt16,HumanFortress.Core.Content.TerrainKind)
member.summary: Get display info for a material ID and terrain kind combination.

member.name: T:HumanFortress.Core.Content.TerrainKind
member.summary: Terrain types from TILE_SPEC.md. Must match HumanFortress.Simulation.Tiles.TerrainKind

member.name: T:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.AliasResolver
member.summary: Resolves aliases and handles content migrations
Load aliases from definitions

member.name: M:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.AliasResolver.ResolveMaterialName(System.String,System.Nullable{HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.ContentVersion})
member.summary: Resolve a material name through aliases

member.name: M:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.AliasResolver.ResolveTerrainName(System.String,System.Nullable{HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.ContentVersion})
member.summary: Resolve a terrain kind name through aliases

member.name: M:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.AliasResolver.ApplyMaterialMigrations(System.String,HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.ContentVersion)
member.summary: Apply material migrations from a specific version

member.name: M:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.AliasResolver.ApplyTerrainMigrations(System.String,HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.ContentVersion)
member.summary: Apply terrain migrations from a specific version

member.name: M:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.AliasResolver.ApplyMigrationRule(System.String,HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.MigrationRule,HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.ContentMigration)
member.summary: Apply a single migration rule

member.name: M:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.AliasResolver.GetActiveMaterialAliases(System.Nullable{HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.ContentVersion})
member.summary: Get all active aliases for a given version

member.name: M:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.AliasResolver.IsDeprecated(System.String,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Check if an alias is deprecated

member.name: M:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.AliasResolver.GetDeprecationWarning(System.String,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Get deprecation warning for an alias

member.name: M:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.AliasResolver.Clear
member.summary: Clear all aliases and migrations

member.name: T:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.AliasDefinition
member.summary: Definition of an alias for backward compatibility

member.name: T:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.ContentMigration
member.summary: Definition of a content migration between versions

member.name: T:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.MigrationRule
member.summary: A single migration rule

member.name: T:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.BiomeTemplateDefinition
member.summary: Complete definition of a biome generation template

member.name: T:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.BiomeTemplateRegistry
member.summary: Registry for biome generation templates

member.name: M:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.BiomeTemplateRegistry.LoadTemplates(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.BiomeTemplateDefinition},HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.ContentVersion)
member.summary: Load templates from definitions

member.name: M:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.BiomeTemplateRegistry.AddTemplate(HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.BiomeTemplateDefinition)
member.summary: Add a template to the registry

member.name: M:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.BiomeTemplateRegistry.GetTemplate(System.String)
member.summary: Get template by ID

member.name: M:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.BiomeTemplateRegistry.SelectTemplate(HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.BiomeParameters)
member.summary: Select best matching template for given parameters

member.name: M:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.BiomeTemplateRegistry.SelectTemplateById(System.String)
member.summary: Select template by explicit ID with fallback

member.name: M:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.BiomeTemplateRegistry.GetTemplatesForClimate(System.Single,System.Single)
member.summary: Get templates for a specific climate

member.name: M:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.BiomeTemplateRegistry.GetDefaultTemplate
member.summary: Get default/fallback template

member.name: M:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.BiomeTemplateRegistry.GetTemplateIds
member.summary: Get all template IDs for save snapshot

member.name: M:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.BiomeTemplateRegistry.GetTemplateCount
member.summary: Get template count

member.name: M:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.BiomeTemplateRegistry.GetAllTemplates
member.summary: Get all templates

member.name: M:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.BiomeTemplateRegistry.Clear
member.summary: Clear the registry

member.name: T:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.ConstructionDefinition
member.summary: Construction definition for on-site built structures (walls, floors, workshops, etc.) Quality is always 0 for constructions (per PLACEABLE_SPEC §15.2)

member.name: P:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.ConstructionDefinition.Id
member.summary: Unique construction definition ID (e.g., "core_construction_wall_stone")

member.name: P:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.ConstructionDefinition.Name
member.summary: Display name (material-agnostic, e.g., "Stone Wall")

member.name: P:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.ConstructionDefinition.Category
member.summary: Primary category for UI grouping (walls, floors, stairs, doors, workshops, defenses)

member.name: P:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.ConstructionDefinition.BuildTimeTicks
member.summary: Base construction time in ticks (modified by worker skill)

member.name: P:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.ConstructionDefinition.MaterialCosts
member.summary: Required materials for construction

member.name: P:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.ConstructionDefinition.SkillRequired
member.summary: Optional skill tag required for construction (e.g., "skill_masonry", "skill_carpentry")

member.name: P:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.ConstructionDefinition.PlaceableProfile
member.summary: Placeable properties (footprint, effects, passability) Effects are fixed (no quality modifier for constructions)

member.name: T:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.MaterialCost
member.summary: Material cost entry for constructions Can specify either a tag (e.g., "block", "bar") or a specific item definition ID

member.name: P:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.MaterialCost.Tag
member.summary: Material tag filter (e.g., "block", "bar", "log") - mutually exclusive with DefId

member.name: P:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.MaterialCost.DefId
member.summary: Specific item definition ID (e.g., "core_item_component_mechanism") - mutually exclusive with Tag

member.name: P:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.MaterialCost.Count
member.summary: Number of items required

member.name: T:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.ConstructionRegistry
member.summary: Registry for construction definitions (on-site built structures) Loads from data/core/placeable/*.json (walls.json, floors.json, workshops.json, etc.)

member.name: M:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.ConstructionRegistry.LoadConstructions(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.ConstructionDefinition})
member.summary: Load construction definitions into registry

member.name: M:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.ConstructionRegistry.GetConstruction(System.String)
member.summary: Get construction definition by ID

member.name: M:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.ConstructionRegistry.GetConstructionsByCategory(System.String)
member.summary: Get all construction definitions in a category

member.name: M:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.ConstructionRegistry.GetAllConstructions
member.summary: Get all construction definitions

member.name: M:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.ConstructionRegistry.GetAllCategories
member.summary: Get all categories

member.name: T:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.ConstructionTuning
member.summary: Tuning for L0 construction material costs per tile. Values are counts of items required.

member.name: T:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.ContentRegistry
member.summary: Main content registry that manages all loaded content

member.name: M:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.ContentRegistry.LoadContentAsync(System.String)
member.summary: Load all content from the specified path

member.name: M:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.ContentRegistry.LoadContent(System.String)
member.summary: Load content synchronously (for compatibility)

member.name: M:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.ContentRegistry.LoadSchemasAsync(System.String)
member.summary: Load schemas for validation

member.name: M:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.ContentRegistry.LoadMaterialsAsync(System.String,System.Collections.Generic.Dictionary{System.String,System.Text.Json.JsonDocument},System.Boolean)
member.summary: Load materials from JSON

member.name: M:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.ContentRegistry.ParseMaterialDefinition(System.Text.Json.JsonElement)
member.summary: Parse a material definition from JSON using the new v4 parser

member.name: M:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.ContentRegistry.LoadTerrainKindsAsync(System.String,System.Collections.Generic.Dictionary{System.String,System.Text.Json.JsonDocument})
member.summary: Load terrain kinds from JSON

member.name: M:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.ContentRegistry.LoadBiomeTemplatesAsync(System.String,System.Collections.Generic.Dictionary{System.String,System.Text.Json.JsonDocument})
member.summary: Load biome templates

member.name: M:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.ContentRegistry.LoadGeologyAsync(System.String,System.Collections.Generic.Dictionary{System.String,System.Text.Json.JsonDocument})
member.summary: Load geology prototypes from JSON

member.name: M:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.ContentRegistry.LoadAliasesAsync(System.String,System.Collections.Generic.Dictionary{System.String,System.Text.Json.JsonDocument})
member.summary: Load aliases for migration

member.name: M:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.ContentRegistry.CrossValidateContent
member.summary: Cross-validate content references

member.name: M:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.ContentRegistry.ComputeContentHash
member.summary: Compute overall content hash

member.name: M:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.ContentRegistry.GetSnapshot
member.summary: Get content snapshot for saves

member.name: M:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.ContentRegistry.ApplySnapshot(HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.ContentSnapshot)
member.summary: Apply content snapshot when loading saves

member.name: T:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.ContentValidationResult
member.summary: Content validation result

member.name: T:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.ContentSnapshot
member.summary: Content snapshot for saves

member.name: T:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.ContentVersion
member.summary: Represents a content version with semantic versioning

member.name: M:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.ContentVersion.IsCompatibleWith(HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.ContentVersion)
member.summary: Checks if this version is compatible with a required version using semantic versioning rules (major must match, minor/patch can be higher)

member.name: T:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.GeologyDefinition
member.summary: Defines a tile prototype (combination of TerrainKind + Material) This is the actual "recipe" for creating tiles in the world

member.name: P:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.GeologyDefinition.Id
member.summary: Unique identifier for this geology prototype

member.name: P:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.GeologyDefinition.Name
member.summary: Display name

member.name: P:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.GeologyDefinition.TerrainKind
member.summary: The terrain kind (shape) - references terrain_kinds.json

member.name: P:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.GeologyDefinition.Material
member.summary: The material - references materials.registry.json

member.name: P:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.GeologyDefinition.NavCostBase
member.summary: Per-prototype navigation cost override If null, uses TerrainKind base + Material modifier

member.name: P:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.GeologyDefinition.Opacity
member.summary: Per-prototype opacity override (for line of sight) If null, uses TerrainKind default

member.name: P:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.GeologyDefinition.Tags
member.summary: Tags for this specific combination

member.name: M:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.GeologyDefinition.Validate
member.summary: Validate the geology definition

member.name: M:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.GeologyDefinition.CalculateMoveCost(HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.TerrainKindDefinition,HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.MaterialDefinition)
member.summary: Calculate the final movement cost for this tile prototype

member.name: M:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.GeologyDefinition.IsValidCombination(HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.TerrainKindDefinition,HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.MaterialDefinition)
member.summary: Check if this geology is valid for a given material/terrain combination

member.name: T:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.GeologyRegistry
member.summary: Registry for geology definitions (tile prototypes)

member.name: M:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.GeologyRegistry.LoadPrototypes(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.GeologyDefinition},HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.ContentVersion)
member.summary: Load geology prototypes from definitions

member.name: M:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.GeologyRegistry.AddPrototype(HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.GeologyDefinition)
member.summary: Add a geology prototype to the registry

member.name: M:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.GeologyRegistry.GetPrototype(System.String)
member.summary: Get prototype by ID

member.name: M:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.GeologyRegistry.GetPrototype(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Get prototype by terrain kind and material combination

member.name: M:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.GeologyRegistry.GetOrCreatePrototype(System.String,System.String,HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.TerrainKindDefinition,HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.MaterialDefinition)
member.summary: Find or create a prototype for a terrain/material combination

member.name: M:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.GeologyRegistry.GetPrototypesForTerrainKind(System.String)
member.summary: Get all prototypes for a specific terrain kind

member.name: M:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.GeologyRegistry.GetPrototypesForMaterial(System.String)
member.summary: Get all prototypes for a specific material

member.name: M:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.GeologyRegistry.Clear
member.summary: Clear the registry

member.name: M:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.GeologyRegistry.GetAllPrototypes
member.summary: Get all prototypes

member.name: T:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.MaterialDefinition
member.summary: Material definition per MATERIALS_SPEC v4-min (Fixed-Point Edition). This is the RUNTIME representation with FX integers. Authoring uses human-readable values; compiler scales to FX.

member.name: P:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.MaterialDefinition.Id
member.summary: Runtime numeric ID (assigned by MaterialRegistry on load) Used for fast lookups and compact storage in tiles/items

member.name: P:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.MaterialDefinition.StringId
member.summary: Authoring string ID (from JSON, e.g. "core_mat_metal_iron") Used for content references and human readability

member.name: P:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.MaterialDefinition.DensitySolid
member.summary: Mass source: kg/m³ (== mg/mL numerically) This is the ONLY field stored in physical units (not FX)

member.name: P:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.MaterialDefinition.Mechanics
member.summary: Core mechanics trio (FX integers, 0..100 authoring to 0..FX runtime)

member.name: P:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.MaterialDefinition.ElectricMagic
member.summary: Electricity and magic behavior (FX integers)

member.name: P:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.MaterialDefinition.Economy
member.summary: Economy and aesthetics (FX multipliers)

member.name: P:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.MaterialDefinition.Work
member.summary: Workability and processing difficulty (FX integers)

member.name: P:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.MaterialDefinition.Phase
member.summary: Tech tree metadata (not used in runtime calculations)

member.name: T:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.MaterialMechanics
member.summary: Core mechanical properties (all FX integers) Per SPEC section 2: hardness_edge, toughness_frac, rigidity

member.name: P:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.MaterialMechanics.HardnessEdgeFx
member.summary: Edge hardness (edgeability and edge retention proxy) Authoring: 0..100 (can go greater than 100 for exotic materials) Runtime: FX integer (0..20000 typical, 10000 = 1.0 = 100 percent)

member.name: P:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.MaterialMechanics.ToughnessFracFx
member.summary: Fracture toughness (anti-chipping proxy) Authoring: 0..100 (can go greater than 100) Runtime: FX integer

member.name: P:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.MaterialMechanics.RigidityFx
member.summary: Rigidity/stiffness (modulus proxy, handling, deflection) Authoring: 0..100 (can go greater than 100) Runtime: FX integer

member.name: M:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.MaterialMechanics.GetCenteredSignals
member.summary: Get centered signals for damage calculations (per SPEC section 6) Returns values in range where 0 is neutral (50 percent)

member.name: T:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.MaterialElectricMagic
member.summary: Electric and magic behavior (all FX integers) Per SPEC section 5

member.name: P:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.MaterialElectricMagic.ElectricCategory
member.summary: Electric category (determines resistance) conductor = 0 resistance, insulator = max resistance, semi = half resistance

member.name: P:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.MaterialElectricMagic.ManaConductivityFx
member.summary: Mana conductivity (FX integer) Authoring: 0..200 (100 = neutral) Runtime: FX (10000 = neutral, less than 10000 gives arcane resist, greater than 10000 amplifies magic)

member.name: M:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.MaterialElectricMagic.GetElectricResistFx(System.Int32)
member.summary: Compute electric resistance (FX) based on category Per SPEC section 5

member.name: M:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.MaterialElectricMagic.GetSpellAmplificationFx
member.summary: Compute spell amplification multiplier (FX) Per SPEC section 5: clamped between 0.25x and 2.0x

member.name: M:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.MaterialElectricMagic.GetArcaneResistFx(System.Int32)
member.summary: Compute arcane resistance (FX) Per SPEC section 5: If mana less than FX, converts deficit into resistance

member.name: T:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.MaterialEconomy
member.summary: Economy and aesthetics (all FX multipliers) Per SPEC section 9

member.name: P:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.MaterialEconomy.ValueMulFx
member.summary: Value multiplier (FX) Authoring: default 1.0 Runtime: FX (10000 = 1.0×)

member.name: P:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.MaterialEconomy.BeautyMulFx
member.summary: Beauty multiplier (FX) Authoring: default 1.0 Runtime: FX

member.name: T:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.MaterialWork
member.summary: Workability and processing difficulty (all FX) Per SPEC section 8

member.name: P:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.MaterialWork.Forgeable
member.summary: Capability flags (recipe gates, not numeric)

member.name: P:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.MaterialWork.ProcessDifficultyMulFx
member.summary: Processing difficulty multiplier (FX) Authoring: default 1.0; greater than 1 means slower/harder/higher skill Runtime: FX (10000 = 1.0x, 15000 = 1.5x harder) Affects mining/chopping/crafting time and skill DC

member.name: T:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.MaterialNavigation
member.summary: Navigation modifiers (per MATERIALS_DATA_CONTRACT) Materials provide ONLY numeric modifiers, NEVER legality booleans

member.name: P:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.MaterialNavigation.MoveCostAdd
member.summary: Additive move cost modifier (integer, not FX) Recommended range: -50..+50

member.name: P:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.MaterialNavigation.FrictionMulFx
member.summary: Friction multiplier (FX) Recommended range: ~8000..12000 (0.8× to 1.2×)

member.name: P:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.MaterialNavigation.HazardLevel
member.summary: Hazard level (integer 0..10)

member.name: P:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.MaterialNavigation.HazardType
member.summary: Hazard type (optional if hazardLevel = 0) heat, cold, acid, shock, miasma, etc.

member.name: T:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.MaterialParser
member.summary: Parser for material definitions - supports both authoring and runtime formats Per MATERIALS_SPEC v4-min: authoring uses human-readable values, runtime uses FX integers

member.name: M:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.MaterialParser.ParseMaterial(System.Text.Json.JsonElement,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Parse material from JSON element (auto-detects authoring vs runtime format)

member.name: M:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.MaterialParser.InferCategoryFromTags(System.Collections.Generic.HashSet{System.String})
member.summary: Infer material category from tags for backward compatibility

member.name: T:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.MaterialRegistry
member.summary: Registry for all material definitions loaded from JSON

member.name: F:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.MaterialRegistry.None
member.summary: Reserved material IDs

member.name: M:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.MaterialRegistry.LoadMaterials(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.MaterialDefinition},HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.ContentVersion)
member.summary: Load materials from definitions

member.name: M:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.MaterialRegistry.AddMaterial(HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.MaterialDefinition)
member.summary: Add a material to the registry

member.name: M:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.MaterialRegistry.AddReservedMaterials
member.summary: Add reserved materials that always exist

member.name: M:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.MaterialRegistry.GetMaterial(System.UInt16)
member.summary: Get material by ID

member.name: M:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.MaterialRegistry.GetMaterial(System.String)
member.summary: Get material by name (checks aliases too)

member.name: M:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.MaterialRegistry.GetMaterialId(System.String)
member.summary: Get material ID by name

member.name: M:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.MaterialRegistry.ResolveMaterial(System.String,System.UInt16)
member.summary: Resolve material name to ID with fallback

member.name: M:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.MaterialRegistry.GetMaterialsByCategory(System.String)
member.summary: Get all materials in a category

member.name: M:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.MaterialRegistry.GetMaterialsByTag(System.String)
member.summary: Get all materials with a tag

member.name: M:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.MaterialRegistry.HasMaterial(System.UInt16)
member.summary: Check if material exists

member.name: M:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.MaterialRegistry.GetNameToIdSnapshot
member.summary: Get a snapshot of name to ID mappings for saves

member.name: M:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.MaterialRegistry.ApplyAliases(System.Collections.Generic.Dictionary{System.String,System.String})
member.summary: Apply aliases for save migration

member.name: M:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.MaterialRegistry.ResolveStringId(System.String)
member.summary: Resolve material string ID to numeric ID (supports aliases)

member.name: M:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.MaterialRegistry.Clear
member.summary: Clear the registry

member.name: M:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.MaterialRegistry.ComputeContentHash
member.summary: Compute content hash for save compatibility checking

member.name: T:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.MaterialSelectionService
member.summary: Reusable selector/cache for user-chosen build materials. Stores last-used material ID per category key (e.g., "l0.floor", "l2.bed"). Does not fetch items from world; only resolves preference and provides a fast cache.

member.name: T:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.PlaceableProfile
member.summary: Placeable profile for items and constructions. Defines footprint, passability, effects, and placement rules. Shared between ItemDefinition.PlaceableProfile and ConstructionDefinition.PlaceableProfile.

member.name: P:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.PlaceableProfile.Footprint
member.summary: Footprint dimensions (no rotation support in MVP)

member.name: P:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.PlaceableProfile.Passability
member.summary: Pathfinding passability mode

member.name: P:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.PlaceableProfile.RequiresFloor
member.summary: Whether this placeable requires a floor tile beneath it

member.name: P:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.PlaceableProfile.ClearanceH
member.summary: Required vertical clearance in tiles above the footprint

member.name: P:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.PlaceableProfile.BlocksLight
member.summary: Whether this placeable blocks light propagation

member.name: P:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.PlaceableProfile.Tags
member.summary: Tags for placeable-specific filtering (e.g., room requirements, "furniture_bed", "workshop_masonry")

member.name: P:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.PlaceableProfile.Effects
member.summary: Environmental effects (base values, quality modifier applied at install time for items)

member.name: T:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.Footprint
member.summary: Footprint dimensions in tiles (W×D×H) No rotation support in MVP - all placeables have fixed orientation

member.name: P:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.Footprint.W
member.summary: Width (x-axis, tiles)

member.name: P:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.Footprint.D
member.summary: Depth (y-axis, tiles)

member.name: P:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.Footprint.H
member.summary: Height (z-axis, tiles)

member.name: T:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.PassabilityMode
member.summary: Passability mode for pathfinding integration

member.name: F:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.PassabilityMode.Blocking
member.summary: Wall-like, completely blocks movement and line of sight

member.name: F:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.PassabilityMode.Nonblocking
member.summary: Passable, can walk through freely

member.name: F:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.PassabilityMode.Doorway
member.summary: Door-like, passability depends on open/closed state (requires DoorState component)

member.name: T:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.EffectsBlock
member.summary: Environmental effects block (beauty, comfort, light, heat) Base values from definition, modified by quality for installable items

member.name: P:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.EffectsBlock.Beauty
member.summary: Beauty value (discrete integer, can be negative)

member.name: P:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.EffectsBlock.Comfort
member.summary: Comfort value (discrete integer, non-negative)

member.name: P:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.EffectsBlock.LightLumen
member.summary: Light output in lumens (discrete integer)

member.name: P:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.EffectsBlock.HeatW
member.summary: Heat output in watts (discrete integer)

member.name: T:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.PlaceableTuning
member.summary: Tunable placeable system parameters from tuning.placeable.json. Per PLACEABLE_SPEC.md.

member.name: P:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.PlaceableTuning.BeautyPerTier
member.summary: Beauty effect per quality tier (-3 to +3)

member.name: P:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.PlaceableTuning.ComfortPerTier
member.summary: Comfort effect per quality tier (-3 to +3)

member.name: P:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.PlaceableTuning.MinTier
member.summary: Minimum quality tier

member.name: P:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.PlaceableTuning.MaxTier
member.summary: Maximum quality tier

member.name: P:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.PlaceableTuning.DefaultMaxHP
member.summary: Default maximum hit points for placeables

member.name: P:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.PlaceableTuning.HPPerVolumeML
member.summary: Hit points per milliliter of volume

member.name: P:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.PlaceableTuning.MaterialHPMultiplier
member.summary: Material HP multipliers (stone=2.0, metal=3.0, wood=1.0, default=1.0)

member.name: P:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.PlaceableTuning.ConditionThresholds
member.summary: Condition state thresholds (pristine=1.0, good=0.9, worn=0.7, etc.)

member.name: P:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.PlaceableTuning.InstallTimeBaseTicks
member.summary: Base installation time in ticks

member.name: P:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.PlaceableTuning.DeconstructTimeBaseTicks
member.summary: Base deconstruction time in ticks

member.name: P:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.PlaceableTuning.MaterialRecoveryRate
member.summary: Material recovery rate on deconstruction (0.0 to 1.0)

member.name: P:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.PlaceableTuning.PreserveItemOnUninstall
member.summary: Whether to preserve item properties on uninstall

member.name: P:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.PlaceableTuning.ConstructionQualityAlwaysZero
member.summary: Whether construction quality tier is always zero

member.name: P:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.PlaceableTuning.SkillXPPerBuildTick
member.summary: Skill XP gained per build tick

member.name: P:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.PlaceableTuning.DoorDefaultLocked
member.summary: Default locked state for doors

member.name: P:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.PlaceableTuning.DoorDefaultOpen
member.summary: Default open state for doors

member.name: P:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.PlaceableTuning.DoorOpenCostTicks
member.summary: Time cost to open a door (ticks)

member.name: P:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.PlaceableTuning.DoorCloseCostTicks
member.summary: Time cost to close a door (ticks)

member.name: P:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.PlaceableTuning.DoorClosedBlocksMovement
member.summary: Whether closed doors block movement

member.name: P:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.PlaceableTuning.CheckFullFootprint
member.summary: Whether to check full footprint for collision (vs. just anchor cell)

member.name: P:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.PlaceableTuning.RequireWalkableTiles
member.summary: Whether placement requires all tiles to be walkable

member.name: P:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.PlaceableTuning.AllowOverlapExternalRefs
member.summary: Whether to allow overlap with external references

member.name: P:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.PlaceableTuning.CrossChunkValidation
member.summary: Whether to perform cross-chunk validation

member.name: P:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.PlaceableTuning.Default
member.summary: Get default tuning values.

member.name: M:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.PlaceableTuning.LoadFromContent
member.summary: Load tuning from content registries (tuning.placeable.json). Falls back to defaults.

member.name: T:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.TerrainKindDefinition
member.summary: Complete definition of a terrain kind (shape/form) loaded from JSON

member.name: T:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.TerrainKindRegistry
member.summary: Registry for terrain kind definitions loaded from JSON

member.name: M:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.TerrainKindRegistry.LoadTerrainKinds(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.TerrainKindDefinition},HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.TerrainBitLayout,HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.ContentVersion)
member.summary: Load terrain kinds from definitions

member.name: M:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.TerrainKindRegistry.AddTerrainKind(HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.TerrainKindDefinition)
member.summary: Add a terrain kind to the registry

member.name: M:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.TerrainKindRegistry.GetKind(System.Byte)
member.summary: Get terrain kind by ID

member.name: M:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.TerrainKindRegistry.GetKind(System.String)
member.summary: Get terrain kind by name

member.name: M:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.TerrainKindRegistry.GetKindId(System.String)
member.summary: Get terrain kind ID by name

member.name: M:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.TerrainKindRegistry.ResolveKind(System.String,System.Byte)
member.summary: Resolve terrain kind name to ID with fallback

member.name: M:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.TerrainKindRegistry.IsMaterialAllowed(HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.TerrainKindDefinition,System.String)
member.summary: Check if a material can have a specific terrain kind

member.name: M:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.TerrainKindRegistry.GetKindsForMaterial(System.String)
member.summary: Get all terrain kinds that allow a material category

member.name: M:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.TerrainKindRegistry.BuildNavigationMask(HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.TerrainKindDefinition)
member.summary: Build navigation mask from terrain kind

member.name: M:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.TerrainKindRegistry.Clear
member.summary: Clear the registry

member.name: T:HumanFortress.Core.Content.Registry.TerrainBitLayout
member.summary: Describes the bit layout of the TerrainBits field

member.name: T:HumanFortress.Core.Content.ZoneDefinitionData
member.summary: Content-driven zone definition data (DTO for JSON loading).

member.name: T:HumanFortress.Core.Events.EventBus
member.summary: Thread-safe event bus implementation.

member.name: T:HumanFortress.Core.Events.IEventBus
member.summary: Event bus for post-commit game events per GAME_ARCHITECTURE.md. Events are fired after write phase completes.

member.name: M:HumanFortress.Core.Events.IEventBus.Publish``1(``0)
member.summary: Publish an event to all subscribers.

member.name: M:HumanFortress.Core.Events.IEventBus.Subscribe``1(System.Action{``0})
member.summary: Subscribe to events of a specific type.

member.name: M:HumanFortress.Core.Events.IEventBus.Unsubscribe``1(System.Action{``0})
member.summary: Unsubscribe from events.

member.name: T:HumanFortress.Core.Events.IGameEvent
member.summary: Base interface for all game events.

member.name: P:HumanFortress.Core.Events.IGameEvent.Tick
member.summary: Tick when the event occurred.

member.name: P:HumanFortress.Core.Events.IGameEvent.EventType
member.summary: Event type for debugging.

member.name: T:HumanFortress.Core.Random.DeterministicGuidGenerator
member.summary: Deterministic GUID generation for placeable instances. Uses DeterministicRng to ensure replay consistency per DETERMINISM_CI.md.

member.name: M:HumanFortress.Core.Random.DeterministicGuidGenerator.Generate(HumanFortress.Core.Random.DeterministicRng)
member.summary: Generate deterministic GUID from RNG state. GUID structure: 128-bit (16 bytes) = 4x uint32

member.name: M:HumanFortress.Core.Random.DeterministicGuidGenerator.GenerateFromPosition(System.UInt64,System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Generate deterministic GUID from position-based seed. Seed = tickSeed ^ HashPosition(x, y, z)

member.name: M:HumanFortress.Core.Random.DeterministicGuidGenerator.HashPosition(System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Hash position to 64-bit value for seed generation. Uses FNV-1a hash algorithm for good distribution.

member.name: T:HumanFortress.Core.Random.DeterministicRng
member.summary: Deterministic random number generator using xoshiro128++ algorithm. Fast, high-quality PRNG suitable for games per DETERMINISM_CI.md.

member.name: M:HumanFortress.Core.Random.DeterministicRng.#ctor(System.UInt64)
member.summary: Initialize with a seed value.

member.name: M:HumanFortress.Core.Random.DeterministicRng.Next
member.summary: Get the next random 32-bit value.

member.name: M:HumanFortress.Core.Random.DeterministicRng.NextInt(System.Int32)
member.summary: Get a random integer in range [0, max).

member.name: M:HumanFortress.Core.Random.DeterministicRng.NextInt(System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Get a random integer in range [min, max).

member.name: M:HumanFortress.Core.Random.DeterministicRng.NextFloat
member.summary: Get a random float in range [0, 1).

member.name: M:HumanFortress.Core.Random.DeterministicRng.NextDouble
member.summary: Get a random double in range [0, 1).

member.name: M:HumanFortress.Core.Random.DeterministicRng.NextBool
member.summary: Get a random boolean.

member.name: M:HumanFortress.Core.Random.DeterministicRng.GetState
member.summary: Save RNG state for deterministic replay.

member.name: M:HumanFortress.Core.Random.DeterministicRng.SetState(HumanFortress.Core.Random.RngState)
member.summary: Restore RNG state from save.

member.name: T:HumanFortress.Core.Random.RngState
member.summary: Serializable RNG state.

member.name: T:HumanFortress.Core.Random.RngStreamManager
member.summary: Manages named RNG streams for deterministic simulation per DETERMINISM_CI.md. Each system gets its own stream to prevent coupling.

member.name: M:HumanFortress.Core.Random.RngStreamManager.GetStream(System.String)
member.summary: Get or create a named RNG stream.

member.name: T:HumanFortress.Core.Random.RngStreamManager.StreamNames
member.summary: Standard stream names per system.

member.name: M:HumanFortress.Core.Random.RngStreamManager.SaveStates
member.summary: Save all stream states for deterministic replay.

member.name: M:HumanFortress.Core.Random.RngStreamManager.RestoreStates(System.Collections.Generic.Dictionary{System.String,HumanFortress.Core.Random.RngState})
member.summary: Restore stream states from save.

member.name: T:HumanFortress.Core.Simulation.DiffOp
member.summary: Diff-log operation for tile/entity modifications per DIFF_LOG_AND_MERGE_STRATEGIES.md. All write operations must go through this system for determinism.

member.name: P:HumanFortress.Core.Simulation.DiffOp.SortKey
member.summary: Stable sort key for deterministic merge order.

member.name: T:HumanFortress.Core.Simulation.DiffLog
member.summary: Collects and merges diff operations for a single tick.

member.name: M:HumanFortress.Core.Simulation.DiffLog.AddOp(HumanFortress.Core.Simulation.DiffOp)
member.summary: Add an operation to the log. Thread-safe for Write phase.

member.name: M:HumanFortress.Core.Simulation.DiffLog.MergeAndSort
member.summary: Merge and apply all operations in deterministic order.

member.name: M:HumanFortress.Core.Simulation.DiffLog.Clear
member.summary: Clear the log for the next tick.

member.name: T:HumanFortress.Core.Simulation.UpdateOrder
member.summary: Defines the authoritative update order for simulation systems per UPDATE_ORDER.md. All systems execute in Read phase (parallel), then Write phase (serialized).

member.name: T:HumanFortress.Core.Simulation.UpdateOrder.Priority
member.summary: System priorities for execution order. Lower values execute first.

member.name: T:HumanFortress.Core.Simulation.UpdateOrder.SystemId
member.summary: System identifiers for debugging and error reporting.

member.name: T:HumanFortress.Core.Time.ITick
member.summary: Represents a tickable system that participates in the fixed-step simulation loop.

member.name: M:HumanFortress.Core.Time.ITick.ReadTick(System.UInt64)
member.summary: Called during the read phase of a tick. Systems can read state but cannot modify it. Multiple systems may execute this phase in parallel.

member.name: M:HumanFortress.Core.Time.ITick.WriteTick(System.UInt64)
member.summary: Called during the write phase of a tick. Systems can modify state through the diff-log or chunk-actor patterns only.

member.name: P:HumanFortress.Core.Time.ITick.Priority
member.summary: Priority for system execution order within a phase. Lower values execute first.

member.name: P:HumanFortress.Core.Time.ITick.SystemId
member.summary: System identifier for debugging and error reporting.

member.name: T:HumanFortress.Core.Time.TickScheduler
member.summary: Fixed-step tick scheduler implementing the authoritative UPDATE_ORDER. Runs at 50 TPS (20ms per tick) with read-parallel/write-serialized execution.

member.name: P:HumanFortress.Core.Time.TickScheduler.CurrentTick
member.summary: Current simulation tick number.

member.name: P:HumanFortress.Core.Time.TickScheduler.IsRunning
member.summary: Whether the simulation is currently running.

member.name: P:HumanFortress.Core.Time.TickScheduler.TargetTPS
member.summary: Target ticks per second (fixed at 50).

member.name: P:HumanFortress.Core.Time.TickScheduler.IsPaused
member.summary: Whether the simulation is currently paused.

member.name: P:HumanFortress.Core.Time.TickScheduler.SpeedMultiplier
member.summary: Current speed multiplier (0.25x - 8x).

member.name: M:HumanFortress.Core.Time.TickScheduler.Pause
member.summary: Pause the simulation. Does not affect command queue.

member.name: M:HumanFortress.Core.Time.TickScheduler.Resume
member.summary: Resume the simulation from pause.

member.name: M:HumanFortress.Core.Time.TickScheduler.TogglePause
member.summary: Toggle pause state.

member.name: M:HumanFortress.Core.Time.TickScheduler.SetSpeed(System.Single)
member.summary: Set simulation speed multiplier. Clamped to [0.25, 8.0] range.

member.name: M:HumanFortress.Core.Time.TickScheduler.CycleSpeedUp
member.summary: Cycle through predefined speed levels: 0.25x, 0.5x, 1x, 2x, 4x, 8x.

member.name: M:HumanFortress.Core.Time.TickScheduler.CycleSpeedDown
member.summary: Cycle through predefined speed levels (reverse).

member.name: M:HumanFortress.Core.Time.TickScheduler.RegisterSystem(HumanFortress.Core.Time.ITick)
member.summary: Register a system to participate in the tick loop.

member.name: M:HumanFortress.Core.Time.TickScheduler.Start
member.summary: Start the fixed-step simulation loop.

member.name: M:HumanFortress.Core.Time.TickScheduler.Stop
member.summary: Stop the simulation loop after the current tick completes.

member.name: M:HumanFortress.Core.Time.TickScheduler.ExecuteSingleTick
member.summary: Execute a single tick synchronously (for testing/replay).
